nameMeanings.name: Langton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the many places called with Old English lang ‘long’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, such as Langton Hall in Kirkby in Ashfield (Nottinghamshire), Langton Lodge in Hendon (Middlesex), Langton (Yorkshire), East and West Langton (Leicestershire), Langton by Horncastle, Langton by Spilsby, and Langton by Wragby (all in Lincolnshire), and Lanton (Northumb). Compare Lankton , Lanton , and Longton .

variants.name: Lankton
meanings: History: Thomas Lankton, born c. 1714 in Hartford County, CT, died 1783 in Litchfield County, CT, was a son of Joseph Langton.

variants.name: Longton
meanings: habitational name from Longton in Lancashire. The placename derives from Old English lang ‘long’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Nelson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which originated in the Middle Ages as either a patronymic from   Neil   or a metronymic from   Nell .  Use as a given name probably began as a tribute to the British admiral  Lord Nelson  (  1758–1805 ), the victor of the Battle of Trafalgar;  see also  Horatio .  It is, however, now much more common in the United States than in Britain, its popularity there in the 1930s and 40s no doubt having been influenced by the American film actor and singer  Nelson Eddy  (  1901–67 ). Nowadays the name is universally associated with the South African statesman  Nelson Mandela  (  b. 1918 ).

historicEvents.title: The Tuscarora War
historicEvents.description: The British, Dutch, and German settlers fought with the Tuscarora Native Americans, from September 22, 1711- February 11, 1715, in North Carolina.
North Carolina is the location of Cape Hatteras, sometimes called the Graveyard of the Atlantic- many shipwrecks have occurred nearby.

historicEvents.title: Becomes a Royal Colony
historicEvents.description: July 25, 1729, North Carolina became a royal colony, when the colony was sold to King George II.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Anchor-Carolina Becomes North and South Carolina

en.lifeSummary: Nelson Langton was born about 1655, in Beaufort, North Carolina, British Colonial America. He had at least 1 daughter with Mrs Nelson Langston.
